title: alyssum campestre subsp. collinum
intro: Alyssum campestre subsp. collinum, also known as field candytuf or small-flowered candytuft, is a plant species within the Alyssum campestre complex, characterized by its small white flowers and blue-green foliage. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Alyssum campestre subsp. collinum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
